programme.title: This Week In Spaceflight
programme.desc: NASASpaceflight.com's weekly spaceflight update. A Falcon 9 second stage is stuck in orbit, NASA is looking for a new partner for the VIPER lunar rover, and an asteroid may have a 2.3% chance of hitting Earth in 2032! Meanwhile, Firefly’s Blue Ghost is preparing for lunar orbit insertion, and Astrolab’s FLIP rover joins Astrobotic’s Griffin-1 mission to the Moon.

programme.title: Starbase Update
programme.desc: NASASpaceflight.com's weekly look at the happenings around Starbase. This week at Starbase, Pad B takes a giant leap forward with the installation of chopsticks, the digging of the flame trench, and the tank farm coming together. Meanwhile, Booster 15 moves closer to static fire, Ship 34 nears testing, and Massey’s test site sees preparations for Block 2 hardware. Plus, our flyover reveals SpaceX’s upgraded pad design, showing how lessons from Pad A are shaping Starbase’s future.

programme.title: What If: Cosmos
programme.sub-title: The Sun’s Fate: Dying, Dimming, or Doubling
programme.desc: Explore the possible futures of our Sun: Will it die, dim, or double? Unravel the mysteries of solar destiny!

programme.title: What If: Cosmos
programme.desc: Zoom into the fast-paced dangers of hypervelocity stars and objects. Discover how they disrupt galaxies and pose cosmic threats!

programme.title: Hacksmith: Make It Real
programme.sub-title: Fortnite Sword, Halo Helmet, Ice Blaster
programme.desc: Dive into iconic gaming gear, from the mighty Fortnite sword to the legendary Halo helmet and the chilling ice blaster—explore the technology and creativity behind these fan-favorite tools!

programme.title: Hacksmith: Make It Real
programme.sub-title: E-Foil Broom, Bone Claws, Predator Vision
programme.desc: Discover futuristic and fierce gadgets, from the e-foil broom to bone claws and Predator’s iconic vision mode—explore how these tools would revolutionize survival and adventure!

programme.title: Masters Of The Wild
programme.desc: Animals adapt to hostile landscapes using specialized bodies, diets, and behaviors, such as scorpions

programme.title: Germany's Wild Wolves
programme.desc: Germany's Wild Wolves challenges modern myths about wolves, revealing their social structures

programme.title: Wild Slovakia
programme.desc: Wild Slovakia is a documentary film showcasing Slovakia's diverse wildlife, habitats, and ecosystems

programme.title: Biebrza - The European Amazon
programme.desc: A unique story about Biebrza - the European Amazon

programme.title: Storks - A Village Rooftop Saga
programme.desc: Ruhstadt, Germany, hosts Germany's largest white stork colony, despite facing damage to roads, roofs

programme.title: Addos Elephants
programme.desc: Protected elephants live and thrive in Addo Elephant National Park.

programme.title: Bama And The Lost Gorillas
programme.desc: Follow the gorilla whisperer, Alfred Bama, as he tries to reunite a Cross River Gorilla with her family.

programme.title: Uakari: Secrets of the English Monkey
programme.desc: Mark Bowler studies and protects the rare Red Uakari monkey in Lago Preto, Peru, focusing on the Aguaje Palm.

programme.title: Equator
programme.desc: The Indonesian archipelago comprises of the world’s largest concentration of coral reefs.

programme.title: Equator
programme.desc: The high slopes of Ecuador’s volcanoes are a cold desert known as the paramo, the ‘inhospitable lands’.

programme.title: Equator

programme.title: CBS Weekend News
programme.desc: Journalist Adriana Diaz anchors the Saturday edition of the CBS news program from Chicago, while Jericka Duncan takes the helm of the Sunday broadcast from New York. The program offers the latest headlines, original reporting and in-depth pieces that add context and perspective to the world's events.

programme.title: 60 Minutes
programme.desc: The oldest and most-watched newsmagazine on television gets the real story on America's most prevalent issues. CBS News correspondents contribute segments to each hourlong episode. Topics range from hard news coverage to politics, lifestyle, pop culture, business, health, and science. The correspondents and contributors include Lesley Stahl, Scott Pelley, Anderson Cooper, Bill Whitaker, Sharyn Alfonsi, Jon Wertheim and Norah O'Donnell .

programme.title: CBS News Sunday Morning
programme.desc: The sparkling notes of a trumpet fanfare and the familiar logo of the sun alert viewers that it's time for CBS's Sunday morning staple. Journalist Jane Pauley helms the show, taking over hosting duties from Charles Osgood, who spent 22 years on the job. A morning talk show, this program airs at a different pace and focuses much of its attention on the performing arts. After a quick update of the day's news and national weather, correspondents offer longer-length segments on a variety of topics, from architecture to ballet to music to pop culture to politics.

programme.title: May the Best Wedding Win
programme.desc: When Tiffany's fiancé calls off their wedding, she's heartbroken that her dream wedding is cancelled. Tiffany convinces an actor to pretend to be her fiancé in exchange for signing him to her agency, and the dream wedding happens for someone.

programme.title: How to Find Forever
programme.desc: Marley is great at organizing other people's engagements but unlucky with love. However, the man who could jeopardize her latest project may also be Mr. Right.

programme.title: Being Erica
programme.desc: Erica looks forward to escaping into her past when she deals with everyone else's problems.

programme.title: Being Erica
programme.sub-title: A River Runs Through It...It Being Egypt
programme.desc: Erica sees a different side of her brother and returns from the past ready to confront her true feelings for Kai.

programme.title: Being Erica
programme.desc: Dr. Tom goes on his own session back to the day he last saw his daughter.

programme.title: Moon Rock for Monday
programme.desc: After an unlikely encounter at a Sydney train station in 1999, a young girl befriends a fugitive teenage boy, and they set off to visit a moon rock the girl believes will heal her.

programme.title: The Mulligan
programme.desc: Paul McAllister seems to have it all, but his life begins to fall apart. Guided by an old golf pro, he learns to do better on and off the green.

programme.title: Love's Playlist
programme.desc: A music executive learns that the anonymous man she's been flirting with online is also a colleague whom she can't stand.

programme.title: The Love Gala
programme.desc: When Kate is given the job of planning her mom's Garden Gala, the biggest fashion event of the year, she has to compromise with the unhelpful head of the Botanical Garden.

programme.title: A Very English Christmas
programme.desc: Christmas in the English countryside isn't in Emma's plans until her sister, Amy, plans her wedding for Christmas eve. Dylan, Amy's Christmas market co-owner, teams up with Emma to bring order to the whirlwind wedding.

programme.title: The PA and the Manhattan Prince
programme.desc: Loyal, quick-thinking Lucy Woods is a personal assistant to the stars, but when Prince Rupert arrives in New York to make preparations for a masked ball, Lucy finds herself falling in love with her new employer.

programme.title: Sue Thomas F.B.Eye
programme.desc: Sue and the team are under the gun after determining seemingly random shootings are the work of a serial sniper.

programme.title: Sue Thomas F.B.Eye
programme.desc: Myles goes on mission for justice after discovering his prized painting is a forgery; a routine traffic accident provides clues about a terrorist plot; Sue encourages Troy (guest star Troy Kotsur) to go to art school.

programme.title: Sue Thomas F.B.Eye
programme.desc: Sue tries to honor a dying woman's request that her daughter's killer be brought to justice; Bobby, Jack and Myles compete over who can draw the highest bid as dream dates in a charity auction.

programme.title: A Snapshot of Forever
programme.desc: Wedding photographer Jessie Brooke's happy life in her small town is changed when a famous Hollywood actor attends her sister's wedding.

programme.title: A Perfect Christmas Pairing
programme.desc: After a bad review from a food critic, renowned chef Grace Cooke returns home for Christmas and reconnects with her roots.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When Sour wishes she could have a birthday all her very own, her twin sister Sweet grants her wish by forgoing celebrating her birthday. But what begins as a day that Sour has always dreamed of quickly becomes a disappointment when she misses her twin. With Strawberry's help, Sour gives her birthday back to Sweet with a special surprise.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: When a comedy of errors causes Orange, Plum and Cherry to arrive late to their campout, they start off their annual tradition of telling tall-tales around the campfire.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: All of Berry Bitty City is gathered around to watch the last snowberrykin melt. As the last bit of snow forms a puddle, a cheer goes up, spring is here! It's now time for the annual BerryFest. But with Princess Berrykin out of town on business, who will be the BerryFest Princess and preside over the festival?! Everyone is abuzz with how to go about choosing the Princess and what her duties will be. Postmaster Bee declares the only fair way to go about it is to hold an election. Everyone agrees.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Strawberry opens her front door to a throng of Berrykins all cheering for the new BerryFest Princess. She is appreciative of their admiration but unsure of exactly what her duties entail. In response the Berrykins bombard her with her duties - major to miniscule. One Berrykin sums it up by telling her she's responsible for presiding over all the activities of the BerryFest to make it the best.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: All of Berry Bitty City is excitedly making their way through the berry batch in search of the elusive Wanderberry. It is to be the dessert at the BerryFest Feast. Everyone is milling about searching for it on their own when Blueberry points out that the rules say the BerryFest Princess is in charge of the search. The girls turn to her for direction and she points them where she thinks the berry might be.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.sub-title: Nothing To Fear But Berries Themselves
programme.desc: Overhearing Blueberry reading a story to the baby Berrykins about the scary Galumphalot, Orange is uncharacteristically shaken. She determines she doesn't want anything scary around her, just relaxation. But while the girls are having a hair-styling party at Lemon's salon, a malfunction at the BerryWorks causes the whole town to go dark. And Orange begins to get really anxious.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: While in the midst of a cake decorating demonstration at the café, the girls are uncharacteristically interrupted by an excited Princess Berrykin. She announces the Berrykins have decided to revive the old tradition of the Berry Bitty Buggy Derby. While the girls enthusiastically recount the stories they've heard about it and ask the Princess questions, Blueberry, carrying enough books to block her view, comes through the door and unknowingly interrupts the Princess.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: The grove around Berry Bitty City is abuzz with activity. The Berrykins are using their sparkles to harvest berries, Berrykin Bloom is in his garden spraying flowers with a special solution to make them larger, and Raspberry is nearby gathering flower petals to make perfume. The wind starts to blow and picks up some of the sparkles. The sparkles float along the wind and get combined with some of Berrykin Bloom's solution.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: It's time again for the Dance-Off between the Big Bitty City Dance Team and the Berry Bitty City Dancers. Plum is stressed out and frazzled because she desperately wants to win. And the Big Bitty City Dancers are a force to be reckoned with. They are elegant and beautifully synchronized. Plum shows the girls a maze of dance steps that she's choreographed in the hopes of creating a winning piece. The problem is, the contest is only 1 week away!

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Raspberry is distraught when she suspects her friends have copied her design on her lantern for the "Glimmerberry Gathering".

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: Lemon is ecstatic. She races out of her salon and goes around to the other girls' houses proclaiming that she "did it!" and beckoning them to join her. They follow her back and are completely dazzled by her newest creation - Glamicures. They are brightly colored, jeweled manicures with tiny microchips inside that play songs when the wearer moves her hand. Each girl lines up to receive her new glamicure and soon there is a cacophony of color and sound.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: While working in her store, Orange overhears the Berrykins laughing and shouting outside. When she goes to the door to investigate, a clump of dandelion fluff gets blown inside chased by three Berrykins. She watches as they head butt the clump around until it floats back toward Orange. Just as it floats close, she gives it a bat sending it outside and the Berrykins happily chasing after it. Wanting in on the fun, she joins them.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: With all the Baby Berrykins running around Berry Bitty City, Princess Berrykin decides to build a Day Care Center and playground. But she's worried because it's dull-looking - all grey metal and brown wood. She asks Strawberry whom might be good to help her out. And two names immediately come to her mind - Lemon and Raspberry! The Princess confesses she's always admired their sense of design and style.

programme.title: Strawberry Shortcake
programme.desc: After returning to the café after a walk with her pets, Strawberry is met by Mr. Longface. He's been waiting for her so that they can get started making treats and decorating for Sadiebug's and Kadiebug's party that afternoon. But Custard and Pupcake have enjoyed their time outside so much that they want to continue playing.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is a big dog and he's the leader of an after school club, called the Squirrels. The Squirrels hear a spooky, echoey voice and other fun adventures.

programme.desc: Duggee is a big dog and he's the leader of an after school club, called the Squirrels. Duggee decides to make an omelette, but there's no eggs and other fun adventures.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: When Gerry is suddenly promoted from sub to full-time teacher, his initial thrill is quickly squashed by the realization that he's back to real teaching responsibilities: namely, marking. Upon meeting a retired teacher who yearns for his old days in the classroom, Gerry concocts a scheme that seems too good to be true. programme.title: Mr. D

programme.title: Hometown Remedy

programme.title: Off The Menu
programme.desc: An unlikely heir to a Mexican fast-food franchise goes ‘cuisine' hunting for the next culinary big thing he can ‘borrow,' and finds himself in a small, dusty New Mexican town where people come from all over to salivate over the culinary treats of a local female chef.

programme.title: The Wedding Planners
programme.desc: Days before her dream princess wedding, Ella gets cold feet and has to decide if Zach is her one and only. (S1 E08)

programme.title: Romance In The Air
programme.desc: After learning her marketing job is on the line, Eden returns to Lake Tahoe where she used to spend her summers growing up. Intending to relax and recharge so she can tackle her job again, she is surprised to find her childhood friend and sweetheart Riley is still living next door. She finds purpose in helping Riley propel his struggling hot air balloon tour company, and also finds herself drawn to him and their dear past. However, Eden sees Riley and Tahoe as temporary, while her life—and boyfriend—back home await her. She can't admit that she and Riley could have a future, even if that's exactly what she wants. When she's willing to take a risk for what her heart wants, her life will take off with her true love.

programme.title: The Bounty Killer
programme.desc: A tenderfoot becomes a bounty hunter in the hopes of making a better future for himself and his new love.

programme.title: The Cariboo Trail
programme.desc: Montanans Jim Redfern and Mike Evans head into Canada's British Columbia via the Cariboo Trail intent to raise cattle and dig for gold but find trouble instead.

programme.title: Cry Blood Apache
programme.desc: Gang of sadistic cowboys let nothing stand in their way in their search for gold.

programme.title: To Hell and Back
programme.desc: Johnny King, a rebellious drifter, sets out to avenge the death of his mentor and friend, Steve, after he has been murdered for his land by the Ward Brothers.

programme.title: White Fang
programme.desc: The wolf dog, White Fang, aids a reporter, a fur trapper, a nun, a young Eskimo boy and his father of ridding a gold mining town of a sleazy crime lord in 1896 Yukon, Canada.

programme.title: Wolves
programme.desc: Wolves: icons of the wilderness, spectacular creatures that live and die by the sword.

programme.title: Uncharted Amazon
programme.desc: Deep in the jungles of Peru, a silky anteater is fighting to stay awake and a mother humming bird is struggling to raise her chick. The seasonal downpours are about to begin and the rainforest's animals must strive to cope with the changing conditions. Through millions of years of evolution they have developed bizarre relationships and unexpected strategies to overcome them, but for all their ingenuity, they were never prepared for the arrival of a new species on the scene.

programme.title: Enchanted Forest
programme.desc: There are few places on the planet more magical than the forest. To spend a day here is nature's way of offering a spa, a bathe in nature. There is enchantment at every turn.

programme.title: The Secret Life of Owls
programme.desc: We all recognize the haunting nighttime hoots of the Great Horned Owl. This powerful predator lives all over the Americas. Yet it's rare to catch a glimpse. The Secret Life of Owls gives us intimate look at this amazing bird of prey and introduces us to some passionate owl experts along the way.

programme.title: Antelope
programme.desc: For some the word ‘antelope' might not incite much interest but look closer and you'll discover a remarkably diverse group of animals, numbering nearly one hundred different species. Some are easy to miss, the little dik-dik being barely the size of a house cat, while eland weigh in at more than half a ton. There are representatives roaming plains from the Mongolian Plateau in Asia to the cape of South Africa. Wherever you find them, these remarkable animals are the unsung heroes of the plains.

programme.title: Nature's Cutest
programme.desc: As we all know the natural world can be tough and challenging at times, but it can also be nothing short of adorable.

programme.title: Penguin Party
programme.desc: From South Africa to Georgia and all across the southern hemisphere, the penguin party never ends!

programme.title: Alpine Lakes
programme.desc: This is a nature documentary, which leads us into the fascinating world of deep mountain lakes. We conquer ice palaces of unsuspected beauty. In the freezers, in which elves, fairies and mountain trolls once did their mischief, we move, using a special breathing technique and with special cameras in search of nocturnal hunters, whose eyes are equipped with residual light amplifying receptors.

programme.title: Bavaria's Alpine Kingdom
programme.desc: There are sparkling palaces from ice and snow, like those found in a fairytale. The icy castles are the phenomenal work of Mother Nature but man had a hand in the magnificent stone castles of the region. The spectacular and histrionic mountains of the Bavarian Alps challenge wildlife and the people who live there and the rocky ramparts of this mountain kingdom are built of limestone and fossil rocks that tell of its origins beneath the sea. Millions of visitors from all over the world come to Bavaria every year to witness the mysticism of the landscape. Kings and Queens, tourist and locals, all are welcomed in the fantastic alpine kingdom. Early summer often comes with the threat of storms that bring floods that scour the land and threaten many creatures. But farmers risk these dangers to take their herds to the highest pastures, where they can graze on the sweetest flower-filled meadows. And later from the high-quality milk produced by their herds, the famers make highly prized cheese. Humankind lives among eagle owls, chamoix, wild cats, stoats and ravens up in these mountains.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: In the heart of Europe lies Switzerland. It's a surprisingly big little country: small in area, but big on natural beauty. And, like its many languages and cultures, its scenic diversity is something really special, too: from mighty Alps, dense forests and lush green valleys, to mysterious moors and roaring waterfalls. Mediterranean lakes and craggy rock landscapes are all close together in one small geographical location. Switzerland has many wild faces – some familiar and some hidden. It's a country that's astonishingly rich in variety, wilderness and scenic wonder. Each episode of the series seeks to do justice to this diversity.

programme.title: The most spectacular change of seasons
programme.desc: This film explores Central Europe and presents the nature from a perspective you have never seen before. Fascinating landscape footage in high-quality HD technology, coupled with spectacular Cineflex aerial views, take us on a journey through the hitherto unknown blazes of colour of the Central European region and its inhabitants. We show you paradise on earth and demonstrate how climate and astronomy determine our life cycle. While seasons change, we visit several different European habitats that - in their diversity, are exemplary of a Central European region. Mountains, water, forests and meadows: we return to each of these habitats at different times of the year and show how the rhythm of life of each of our protagonists follows nature's clock. The four seasons determine all of the coming and passing away a highly complex interplay in the universe of life. We explore the wondrous worlds of the mysterious cycle of nature together with extraordinary people. Breathtaking landscapes enable us to experience the change of seasons at first hand.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.desc: The Bohemian Forest is the source of the Vltava River, home to primeval forests, untouched high moors and bubbling torrents. This archaic stretch of land, which includes the Bavarian Forest, was long considered impenetrable and mysterious. Since the fall of the Iron Curtain, Bavarian border residents have rediscovered new references to this unusual forest landscape for themselves. This is also the case with photo artist Bastian Kalous, who has been documenting his homeland for years with nostalgic-looking Polaroid photographs and is dealing with its eventful history for an illustrated book. Or Elisa Belotti and Marco Heurich, who have dedicated their lives to the lynxes. In order for Europe's largest wild cat to survive in the Bohemian Forest, the lynx researchers fight for every single animal. Iryna Rudenko came to Zwiesel from Ukraine out of a passion for glass as a material to study at the glass school. After graduation, she would like to stay in Bavaria, Anna Hones grew up in Horská Kvilda, the smallest and highest village in the Czech Republic. The village lies just under 1100 meters above sea level, directly on the former border. The farmer, who experienced communism, the fall of the Iron Curtain and the subsequent changes, is now trying to find ways of surviving in this barren region with her children. (S1 E01)

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.desc: Pavel Hubeny, whose life would be unthinkable without the forest, is the head of the Šumava National Park. He is committed to ensuring that a primeval forest can grow for future generations, in which man does not interfere. This vision of a primeval forest is also shared by the forester Claus Bässler, who wants to counteract the extinction of species by having trees felled to create artificial deadwood and thus a new habitat for rare beetles and fungi. On the Vltava River lies the small town of Krumlov, the "Pearl of the Bohemian Forest" with its Baroque theater and fully preserved stage technology from the 17th century. Here the director Zuzana Vrbová enchants people from all over the world with her productions. Apart from agriculture, it was mainly the abundance of forests that ensured the livelihood of the inhabitants of the Bohemian Forest - including that of the carpenter Philipp von Manz, who processed local wood for his customers all over Germany. Veterinarian Judith Gollner, who grew up in the Austrian part of the Bohemian Forest, lived in Vienna for a long time and made a conscious decision to return. She likes the people and is proud of "her farmers' wives" who share her love of the Bohemian Forest. (S1 E02)

programme.title: The Beavers are back
programme.desc: The return of the beavers is one of the greatest success stories of nature conservation in Germany. Hunted for a long time and almost exterminated, today more than 35.000 beavers live in Germany again – and the trend is rising. But how did this happen? One gets to see the mostly nocturnal animals only rarely. Klaus Weißmann has spent more than two years tracking down the shy beavers and documenting their spread in Germany. Which ways do they use? What dangers are they exposed to? Step by step, this film tells the exciting and sometimes odd success story of the likeable rodents.

programme.title: 60 Minutes
programme.desc: The oldest and most-watched newsmagazine on television gets the real story on America's most prevalent issues. CBS News correspondents contribute segments to each hourlong episode. Topics range from hard news coverage to politics, lifestyle, pop culture, business, health, and science. The correspondents and contributors include Lesley Stahl, Scott Pelley, Anderson Cooper, Bill Whitaker, Sharyn Alfonsi, Jon Wertheim and Norah O'Donnell .

programme.title: CBS Weekend News

The two are the latest to fill the roll of weekend anchor, which was most recently represented by Reena Ninan and Eliane Quijano. The program offers the latest headlines, original reporting and in-depth pieces that add context and perspective to the world's events.

programme.title: What Would You Do?
programme.desc: Using hidden cameras, host John Quiñones observes and comments on how ordinary people behave when they are confronted with dilemmas that require them either to take action or to walk by and mind their own business. Various scenarios are created that force people to make split-second -- and often surprising -- decisions, and variations of the situation (such as changing the genders, races or clothing of the people involved) are often featured to see if different reactions are elicited. Examples of situations on the show include a waitress being harassed by her boss, and a drunken valet trying to park cars.

programme.title: The Berry Big Harvest
programme.desc: Finding she has some free time one morning, Strawberry visits Berrykin Bloom who is filled with excitement over his new crop of Berry Bitty fruits and vegetables his garden has never grown so much produce! He can't wait to share his new crop (the perfect bitty size) with Berry Bitty City, so Strawberry volunteers to help him transport the produce to Orange's General Store. Soon Orange's store is overflowing with Berry Bitty Fruits & Veggies. It's the berry first time Orange has not been able to find a “place for everything” in her store.

programme.title: Room at the Top
programme.desc: Strawberry is really touched when her girlfriends and the Berrykins volunteer to help her with the construction of her new marketplace, so when the girls suggest they take charge of designing Strawberry's dream-bedroom to go on the second story, of course Strawberry agrees. While construction is going on, the girls each show Strawberry their individual ideas for the bedroom. Grateful for the support and not wanting to dampen anyone's enthusiasm, Strawberry tells each of them yes (actually, more like not-no).

programme.title: Foods that Smell
programme.desc: Lily and Avery follow their noses to find out what gives foods their amazing aromas. Avery enjoys a super garlicky Caesar salad, and then tries to sniff out a cure for his ghastly garlic breath. Lily makes a splash at the fish market where she gets to the bottom of seafood's suspicious smells. Together, they take on the ultimate olfactory challenge: recreating Matt's Amazing Seafood Chowder, by sense of smell alone!

programme.title: Foods that Melt

Back at the kitchen with Matt, Lily and Avery use a variety to see who can make the of cheeses and fresh veggies the hunt for a simple snack, Avery visits a local cheese shop and discovers there's more best twist on an ooey-gooey melted favourite: grilled cheese sandwiches!

The truth is someone is trying to destroy Greystoke Industries. Tarzan and Jane must figure out who this mystery saboteur is while battling fires, snakes, and sneak attacks. With this Mastermind's henchmen trying to stop them at every turn, our young heroes must take part in a thrilling, multi-country adventure in order to save Greystoke Industries and finally put an end to the criminal enterprise that threatens to put the environment and countless lives - human and animal alike - in danger!

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Going Golfing // Case of the Stolen Spectacles
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that a Rainbow Ball Tournament is about to start soon but he is worried because the field is covered with pokey pods. Thunderbell, Ellie, and Gina are trying to pick them up and clear the field but it is not easy since the pokey pods are so spikey. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that he lost his glasses. Then Ellie tells her she lost her needles, Gina her hat, and Thunderbell his wheels. Who could have taken away all these things?

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Up in the Air // AdDRESSing the Problem
programme.desc: Ling Ling is waiting for Rainbow Ruby, looking worried. It turns out that Mr. Sloth's best friend Chirpee is depressed. Chirpee wants to fly high but is struggling due to his small wings and lack of means to learn how to fly. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that the dress that the paper doll Paige was planning to wear to Kiki's dance party is torn. Paige very much wants to go to the party but she cannot go without a dress.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Ruby's Ranger Adventure // The Show Must Go On
programme.desc: When Ruby and Choco arrive at the village, they find a very worried looking Ling Ling. Gina, Ellie, and Thunderbell went camping in Rainbow Forest but they saw a monster in the forest, got scared and had to run away. // Ruby is struggling to make a doll with her dad. Ruby and Choco get their suitcase and as Ruby swings her umbrella, there they are, in front of Rainbow fountain. Mayor Ling Ling tells her that today is the day of Princess Kiki's ballet performance.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: At Your Service // A Hard Rain
programme.desc: There is a new menu at the restaurant, which is very popular. There is a long line in front of the restaurant but there weren't enough staff members to serve the villagers the food. Rainbow Ruby turns into a waitress using her magic suitcase and serve tables kindly. // Rainbow Village is full of marbles. The marbles that Rainbow Ruby spilled in her room poured down into the village from up above the sky! Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a paramedic.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina, Thunderbell, and Choco try to cheer Jessy up but their efforts are of no use. While talking to Mayor Ling Ling, Rainbow Ruby realizes that today is the day that Jessy first arrived in Rainbow Village! Jessy was upset that her friends forgot such a meaningful day to her. // Ling Ling has caught a bad cold and was sneezing all over the place. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a doctor and cares for him.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Behind the Scenes // Paperdoll in Peril
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried about the play planned for tonight. There are no costumes ready for the villagers so it's hard to figure out which roles they're playing. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a costume designer and designs costumes for her friends. // It is very windy in Rainbow village and Paige, a paper doll, is flying to wherever the wind takes her. She is so light that she is blown away by the wind. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a fire fighter and rescue Paige.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Singing in the Rain // Mail Carrier Mayhem
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried that the villagers are all in low spirits. Ruby thinks of fun games to play but the villagers say that they have already played all of the games too many times. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a singer. // Ling Ling is worried because of the "Secret Present Swapping Party" that Thunderbell is preparing. Thunderbell forgot to send out the invitations. Rainbow Ruby turns into a mail carrier and delivers the invitations.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Kiki wants to become a movie actress but the villagers have no idea how to direct a movie. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to turn into a movie director. She then assigns roles to the village friends. // Ling Ling rushes toward Rainbow Ruby to tell her that a busy villager mom asked him to take care of her three children but he just doesn't have time to look after them. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a babysitter and starts babysitting the children.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.sub-title: Wakey Wakey // Rules of the Road
programme.desc: Princess Kiki is asleep, UP IN THE AIR! According to the note that Kiki left behind, a princess falls under a spell from time to time and in order to break it, they need to play the music box nearby Kiki. But the music box is broken. // Rainbow Ruby and Choco discover cars moving in every direction. Ling Ling asks Rainbow Ruby for help and she uses her magic suitcase to become a police officer.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that today is Noodle Festival day in the village but there is a problem. The problem is that the noodle tree, which grows noodles, decided to take a year off and is not growing any noodles this year. // Ling Ling and the friends are worried about the discouraged Gina. Gina just announced that she will stop doing somersaults anymore because she accidentally hurt her friends while practicing.

Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a plumber and starts fixing the fountain. // Kiki's face has turned purple because she ate magical Plumen berries.
